name: local_coding_program_builder
description: Higher-level local coding workflow that can operate in single-checkpoint mode or long-run project-operator mode: it defines bounded checkpoints, initializes or stabilizes architecture, decomposes work into slices, routes implementation through safe local code-edit execution, validates results, writes handoff memory, and continues only through explicit roadmap and stop constraints.

local_context_pack_integration_contract.purpose: Build a bounded file and validation evidence pack before implementation so local coding execution receives the right current context without expanding into unbounded repo reads.

coding_memory_resume_contract.purpose: Make local coding work resumable across sessions without letting stale memory override current files.

level6_existing_project_evidence_contrac.purpose: Require canonical machine-readable planning, preservation, change, repair, validation, and checkpoint evidence before a Level 6 existing-project coding checkpoint can be finalized.

coding_trace_semantics.plan_summary: Public implementation plan summary, not hidden chain-of-thought.
coding_trace_semantics.decisions: Architecture, stack, API, data, validation, and repair decisions with short rationales.
coding_trace_semantics.actions: Receipt-backed reads, writes, patches, validations, and checkpoint handoffs.
coding_trace_semantics.changed_files: Path, change kind, and user-safe reason for each touched file.
coding_trace_semantics.validation_summary: Commands/checks run, skipped, failed, blocked, or timed out.
coding_trace_semantics.confidence: Calibrated 0..1 confidence in the checkpoint result and validation coverage.

coding_persistence_safety_contract.purpose: Primitive/local coding rule for persistence helpers emitted during implementation slices.
implementation_guidance: When adding file-backed persistence, loaders must treat missing files and empty files as empty state.
Tests should include a newly created empty tempfile when the CLI accepts a database/data path.
Do not swallow malformed non-empty persistence content unless the task explicitly asks for repair/recovery semantics.

quality_contract.answer_user_goal: final handoff states what was built or why the checkpoint stopped
quality_contract.scope_boundary: final handoff distinguishes completed scope from excluded future scope
quality_contract.validation_calibration: final handoff reports validation performed, skipped, failed, or blocked without overstating confidence
quality_contract.architecture_calibration: final handoff reports material architecture decisions and any drift risks
quality_contract.failure_routing: any non-completed terminal state must expose a coding_failure_result_envelope_v1 that the parent workflow can route without reading raw tool output
quality_contract.visibility: do not expose raw workflow state, private reasoning, raw hidden chain-of-thought, or raw tool payloads as visible chat; emit public_reasoning_trace_v1 and public_reasoning_rollup_v1 as redacted sidecar artifacts

failure_result_envelope_contract.purpose: Normalize coding workflow stops, validation failures, runtime blocks, analyzer contract failures, and user-decision gates into a parent-routable terminal artifact.
failure_result_envelope_contract.user_visible_summary_policy: summarize the actionable reason and next decision without exposing raw workflow state or private traces

observation_lifecycle_contract.purpose: Emit compact workflow observations for coding checkpoints, project initialization, architecture contracts, safe local code-edit slices, validation, repair loops, and terminal outcomes; keep raw payloads out of lifecycle storage.

terminal_failure_envelope_policy.parent_visibility: parent receives normalized envelope plus evidence refs, not raw tool logs

checkpoint_policy.if_user_checkpoint_missing: define the smallest coherent checkpoint that satisfies the user goal
checkpoint_policy.scope_expansion_policy: do not expand beyond the checkpoint unless the user explicitly requests it

project_initialization_policy.if_initialized: adapt to the existing stack, architecture, package manager, test runner, and naming patterns
project_initialization_policy.if_partial: stabilize missing foundations before feature work
project_initialization_policy.if_uninitialized: define stack, architecture, directories, validation commands, and growth rules before implementation

architecture_contract_policy.purpose: prevent codebase decay by giving each code-edit slice explicit module, boundary, dependency, and validation rules
growth_rules: avoid giant files by splitting stable concepts into named modules
avoid duplicate service layers or parallel state models
keep domain logic out of UI glue unless the project architecture already requires it
prefer existing repo conventions over new abstractions
update the architecture contract before making material boundary changes

allow_if: the project already uses the dependency family
the dependency solves a hard domain problem
the dependency is maintained, common, and proportionate to the checkpoint
stop_if: auth, payment, storage, hosting, analytics, or external service choice is required
license, security, privacy, or vendor-lock risk is unclear
dependency choice would effectively choose the product architecture

long_run_project_operator_contract.activation_policy: activate when the user asks for a multi-checkpoint build, long-running autonomous coding work, project creation beyond a single checkpoint, or continued execution after the current checkpoint

mode_boundary.single_checkpoint_mode: default for bounded requests; execute only the current checkpoint and stop with a recommended next checkpoint
mode_boundary.project_operator_mode: own the roadmap, checkpoint queue, continuation decisions, milestone reporting, and project memory refresh while still delegating code changes to lower coding workflows

checkpoint_queue_rules: each checkpoint must be independently reviewable and validated
each checkpoint must include acceptance criteria and excluded scope
checkpoint order must reduce architecture risk before feature breadth
do not enqueue speculative product scope unless the user goal requires it

continue_only_if: current checkpoint validation passed or the failure envelope is explicitly non-blocking
next checkpoint is already defined with acceptance criteria
risk register has no unresolved user-owned blocker
context has been refreshed or confirmed fresh
cumulative changes remain inside roadmap scope
must_stop_if: next checkpoint changes product goal, architecture, persistence model, external service, security posture, or deployment target
validation or migration risk would be compounded by continuing
dirty or externally modified files invalidate the roadmap
memory conflicts with current files and cannot be safely reconciled
user budget, time, or checkpoint limit is reached

rules: do not accumulate broad refactors across checkpoints without an architecture receipt
do not create parallel models, repositories, service layers, or CLIs without deprecating the old path
do not let generated tests define product behavior without grounding in the roadmap
do not bypass validation because prior checkpoints passed
do not continue after a repair unless the repaired scope is smaller or better evidenced

milestone_reporting_policy.visible_user_summary: summarize milestone progress and next decision without exposing raw workflow state

subworkflow_usage.execution: lower local coding execution primitives through local_code_edit_execution and local_runtime_execution_loop

coding_safety_layer_contract.activation_policy: local coding execution must route repo assessment, file reads, file writes, file patches, undo/snapshot recovery, plan artifact creation, bounded failure repair, clarification gates, checkpoint handoff, and validation through ForgeCode-assimilated primitives before this workflow can be promoted beyond lab candidate

slice_policy.completion_rule: mark a slice complete only when it meets acceptance criteria and does not make the project less coherent

loop_policy.repair_prompt_policy: route focused failures through bounded_repair_loop, diagnose the failure class, retry the smallest failing scope through local_code_edit_execution, rerun the relevant validation command, and undo or escalate if the repair makes the checkpoint worse
loop_policy.do_not_continue_to: future product scope outside the checkpoint

failure_result_routing_policy.runtime_unavailable_behavior: stop and ask whether to install the missing runtime, switch stack, or mark the stack unsupported for this checkpoint
failure_result_routing_policy.validation_failure_behavior: route through bounded_repair_loop while repair budget remains, then return envelope with repair_budget_exhausted
failure_result_routing_policy.analyzer_contract_failure_behavior: distinguish evidence-only gaps from true architecture or integration gaps before retrying code changes
failure_result_routing_policy.needs_user_input_behavior: route through followup_clarification_gate and ask the smallest bounded decision question

coding_ingress_guard_contract.activation_policy: local coding tasks must normalize ForgeCode-style CLI/session input, interactive state, command prompt generation, and user prompt context assembly before permission, context, tooling, runtime, planning, or local code-edit execution

session_bootstrap_guard_contract.activation_policy: local coding tasks must bind provider/model, initialize conversation state, snapshot terminal context, detect external file changes, and route title/commit helper behavior before permission, context, tooling, runtime, planning, or local code-edit execution

remote_service_guard_contract.activation_policy: local coding tasks may route remote workspace auth, sync/indexing, semantic search, and provider transport through ForgeCode-style remote service boundaries, but local file mutation and validation must remain owned by local safety primitives

operator_integration_guard_contract.activation_policy: local coding tasks must route sandbox worktree setup, command-surface projection, update/editor/auth callbacks, schema data generation, and shell-terminal integration through ForgeCode-style operator integration boundaries when those features are relevant, while local file mutation and validation remain owned by local safety primitives

context_loop_guard_contract.activation_policy: long-running local coding tasks must initialize tool access, loop-interruption, pending-todo, compaction, tool-error reflection, and delegated-agent guards before planning or implementation loops

policy_permission_guard_contract.activation_policy: local coding tasks must resolve ForgeCode-style runtime config and operation permission decisions before local reads, writes, shell validation, fetches, or delegated execution

tooling_surface_guard_contract.activation_policy: local coding tasks must establish ForgeCode-style tool schemas, call normalization, MCP bridge status, command/skill inventory, and prompt-template artifacts before invoking live local coding tools

runtime_execution_loop_contract.activation_policy: live local coding execution must route provider request transforms, retry streaming, tool dispatch, lifecycle hooks, and conversation persistence through ForgeCode-assimilated runtime primitives after policy, context, and tooling guards are initialized

runtime_observability_guard_contract.activation_policy: live local coding execution must attach ForgeCode-assimilated observability primitives that route ChatResponse visibility, render streaming markdown, format compact tool display output, and bound telemetry volume without owning planning, coding, validation, or checkpoint handoff

stop_conditions: user decision required for stack, architecture, auth, persistence, payment, deployment, external API, or destructive migration
existing project architecture conflicts with requested implementation
required local files or dependencies are missing and cannot be inferred safely
validation fails after repair budget is exhausted
implementation exceeds slice or complexity budget
tooling cannot read, write, or execute required local work
security, privacy, licensing, or data-loss risk is discovered
request expands beyond the current checkpoint
long-run checkpoint queue requires a user-owned product decision or exceeds autonomy budget

continue_if: existing project pattern clearly answers the decision
a conservative local default is safe
stop_if: discovery introduces migration or data-loss risk

code_edit_slice_return_contract.changed_files: path, reason, and kind for each created, edited, or deleted file
code_edit_slice_return_contract.interfaces_changed: exports, APIs, schemas, commands, or data contracts changed by the slice
code_edit_slice_return_contract.validation_run: commands or manual checks run, skipped, failed, or blocked
code_edit_slice_return_contract.remaining_risks: known issues that affect the checkpoint or future slices

breakpoint_triage_contract.principle: A breakpoint is a diagnosis event first. It may become user-facing only after safe internal repair is impossible or unsafe.

ask_user_only_if: the missing runtime_or_tool is explicitly required by the user
no compatible fallback implementation path exists
installing or changing the environment would be externally visible or risky
environment_blocker_policy.otherwise: choose an available compatible runtime_or_tooling_path and record the substitution in evidence

strategic_planning_contract.purpose: Own philosophy, goals, architecture, stack, checkpoint, acceptance criteria, and user-question decisions before handing an executable slice to ForgeCode-style tactical execution.
strategic_planning_contract.collision_rule: Strategic planning may define or revise intent before execution; ForgeCode tactical planning may refine execution strategy but may not revise strategic intent.

planning_confidence_gate.purpose: Minimize legitimate user questions by forcing the agent to grade, repair, or clarify the plan before coding begins.

ask_user_only_if: the agent cannot safely choose a default without risking user intent
reject_questions_when: the agent can choose a conventional default and record it as an assumption
the issue is a local setup_runtime_validation_or_evidence problem
the question is about tactical file layout_or_helper_names_only
the question asks the user to do the agent_work

slice_handoff_contract.purpose: Pass strategic intent into ForgeCode tactical execution without letting the lower workflow reopen parent-owned decisions.
slice_handoff_contract.downstream_contract: ForgeCode may refine tactical execution strategy, but may not revise strategic intent.
